title: The One Where Rachel Has A Baby

lines.speaker: Part I Written by
lines.text: Scott Silveri

lines.speaker: Part II Written by
lines.text: David Crane & Marta Kauffman

lines.speaker: Parts I & II Transcribed by
lines.text: Eric Aasen
The Hospital, Ross and Rachel, who's in a wheelchair, are arriving in the waiting room for the maternity ward.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right! (Checking his watch) Yes!! From home to the hospital in under seven minutes! We did it!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (deadpan) Yes, the hard part is truly over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but come on, we're off to a great start aren't we? I knew I'd get you here fast, but this has got to be some kind of a record!
Phoebe and Monica walk in from getting some coffee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh you made it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! (Ross is stunned.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute! How-how the hell did you beat us here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We took a cab. Did you guys walk?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: N... No! We took a cab too, but I did test runs!
Chandler and Joey enter from the vending machines carrying sodas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! You made it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay is there...some kind of magic tunnel to this hospital?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you stay here and talk, I'm gonna go have a baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay. (To the nurse behind the desk.) Umm hi, this is Rachel Green. I'm Ross Geller. We-we called from the car.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Right! We have a semi-private labor room waiting for you. So in just a minute...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (interrupting her) Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa! I'm sorry, semi-private? We (Laughs), we asked for a private room.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Yes, I see that here. Unfortunately we can't guarantee a private room and currently they're all unavailable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Man, if only you'd gotten here sooner. (Ross turns and glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I'm sorry. Semi-private rooms are all we have.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Just give us a second. Ross! (They walk away from the desk.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Give her some money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really think they're out of rooms.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They're not!! Ross, they're just saving them for the important people!! Okay?! What-what if I was the president?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well then we'd be in a lot of trouble, you don't know where any countries are. (Rachel glares at him.) Okay. (He goes over to the desk followed by Rachel.) Uh, say would you umm... Would you mind checking again to see if any umm, private rooms may have (Handing her some money) opened up?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: This is a hospital.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (standing up) Okay. Y'know what? I'd have to say I really don't care for your tone. And this is not the only hospital in this city and we have no problem to-Whoa! (She starts a contraction) Oh gosh! Whoa!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What-what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Contraction. (Sits back down.) Ow-ow! Ow-ow! (Starts breathing heavily.)

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Would you like to see a semi-private room?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it couldn't hurt to look.
Opening Credits
The Semi-Private Labor Room, Rachel is in bed, Ross is fooling around, and Dr. Long is checking on Rachel.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Well you're only two centimeters dilated and we need to get to ten. It'll be a while.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: I'll be back in an hour to check you again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (Dr. Long exits.) Well, I guess we have some time to kill.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, guess so. Whew! Check these out! (He's looking at the stirrups on the other bed in the room and Rachel groans. Ross then hops into the bed and puts his legs into the stirrups.) Never done this before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah well it looks great!
A nurse shows another couple into the room.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Thanks.
They stop when they see Ross who has to struggle to get out of the bed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! Hi, I'm uh Ross. I'm here to ruin this magical day for you.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh no-no, not at all.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Marc Coreger, this is my wife Julie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Julie.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is Rachel. (Points at her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Oh hi Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Hi. Is this your first?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah it is.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Well, little Jamie here is our third. So, if you have questions or you need anything at all, just holler.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's so sweet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm say, I-I opened this earlier (The privacy screen) but let me give you guys some privacy.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: No nonsense! We're all in this together.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Yeah, we are going to share every moment of this with you. And I think we're gonna have some fun.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Hey! Smile! (Points his camera at Ross and Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I really don't want any-(He takes the picture)-Oh! Thank you. Oh. Oh Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here comes another contraction.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Okay, just breathe.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Oh honey, I think I'm having one too!
During the mutual contraction Julie takes a moment to point out they're having a contraction at the same time.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Look at this! (Takes another picture) There we go!
The Waiting Room, the rest of the gang is lounging around.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looking at the clock) Oh wow, three hours and still no baby. Ugh, the miracle of birth sure is a snooze fest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, you wanna see something?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, this is going to be fun. Watch me freak out Chandler. Honey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen uh, I-I've been doing some thinking, and I don't know whether it's because we're here or Rachel's giving birth but umm, I think we should try to have a baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (freaking out) What-what-what's that now?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. I've been thinking about it too, and I, I think we're ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Are you kidding me?! You-you-you think we're ready to have a baby now?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this is fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're ready to have a baby? My boy's all grown up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you said you were ready too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah but I was just screwing with you to try to get your voice all high and weird like mine is now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but haven't you wanted a kid like forever?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just back off mister! Whoa. (Pause) 'Cause I am ready to have a baby. I just want Joey to be the father.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (voice all high and weird) What?! Are you crazy?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's it! Right there! Is all I wanted!
The Semi-Private Labor Room, time lapse, Ross is massaging out a cramp on Rachel's hip as Marc opens up the privacy screen.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: I am so sorry. The doctor insisted on closing the curtain for the exam.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that's very-Really very-very okay.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Julie's cervix is dilated a seven centimeters, that's about four fingers. The doctor let me feel it myself.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Have you felt Rachel's cervix Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (simultaneously as Ross) No, I don't think we'll be doing that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (simultaneously as Rachel) We're not gonna do that.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Well, if you like you can feel Rachel's and then feel mine to compare.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (entering) Am I interrupting?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh yes! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh wait no.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Later.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no-don't! Don't leave me here with these people.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh uh, I'm sorry. (Runs out.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Ross! Ross! Ross! My child has no father!
The Hallway, Ross comes out and hugs Mrs. Geller.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! I'm so glad you're here, but it's gonna be a while. I-I wished you'd called first.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh that's all right, I'm coming back later with your father.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh good.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I actually needed to talk to you before the birth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, what's up?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I brought something that I want to give you, assuming of course that you want it. (She holds up an engagement ring.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ma, you're asking me to marry you?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: This is your grandmother's engagement ring, I want you to give it to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mom no, come on! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Just hear me out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: N-no! Okay? We've been through this! We're not gonna get married just because she's pregnant, okay?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Honestly! Ross, this isn't just some girl you picked up in a bar and humped. A child should have a family.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mom, y'know what? I-I can't deal with this right now. I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Just...think about it. If you don't, I'll talk more about humping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gimmie! (Takes the ring and puts it in his coat pocket as Rachel enters the hallway.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh hi dear!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much for coming. Ross, get in here!
Mrs. Geller leaves as Ross re-enters the room.
The Waiting Room, Ross is explaining to the gang what happened with his mother.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...she came and dragged me out of the labor room to ask me why I'm not with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. (Pause) Why aren't you with Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you kidding? Look, we're not gonna be together just because we're having a baby. Okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But y'know what? It just seems that you two belong together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, stop it! I can't deal with this right now. I have to go have a baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. And with who again? (Ross exits.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: God. He's crazy! Why doesn't he want to be with Rachel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean seriously, she's like the perfect woman. I mean I know she turned me down, but if she hadn't and wanted to be with me, I would take her in my arms and... (Realizes everyone is staring.) I haven't bummed you guys out like this in a while have I?
The Semi-Private Labor Room, Ross is returning to find another couple has taken the place of Marc and Julie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) Hey. Who's that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: New people.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened to the Disgustingtons?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They're having their baby! It's not fair Ross we got here first! Right after you left they wheeled her off into delivery. Oh but not before she gave me a juicy shot of little Jamie just crowning away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Sorry. So uh, how are the new people?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well they have uh, some unusual pet names for each other. Including umm, evil bitch and uh, sick bastard. Oh God oh! Contraction!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! Ow!!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: Are you looking at her?!

lines.speaker: Sick Bastard
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: Don't you look at her you sick bastard!

lines.speaker: Sick Bastard
lines.text: Honey I swear! I wasn't looking at her!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: She's in labor! You like that you sick son of a bitch!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm. Umm, I'm-I'm just gonna-(Closes the privacy screen.)

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: See? See? It was because you were looking fat pervert!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, I'm...I'm sure no one was looking. Just want some privacy. (He closes the screen and stares wide-eyed at Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: You miss your girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just ignore them.
Sick Bastard sits down in a chair that enables him to look around the screen and stare at Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He's looking at me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to him) Hey! You wanna live to see your baby?!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: Don't you talk to my husband like that you stupid bastard!
Ross shrugs his shoulders to Rachel and Sick Bastard closes the screen all the way.
Outside the Nursery, Chandler is looking at the babies as Monica walks up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good God! If you want a baby so bad just go steal it!
The nurse attending to the babies hears this, turns and stares at Chandler. Chandler moves Monica to the side and away from the nurse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is going on with you? Since when are you so crazy about babies?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not crazy about babies. I'm crazy about us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, we've always talked about having babies someday. I'm not saying it has to be right now, but I'm starting to think that we can handle it. We're good. We're really good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We are pretty good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But nothing has to happen until your ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well maybe I'm ready now. I mean, it's a little scary, but maybe it's right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! It's not right! We're not ready to have a kid now!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm kidding. This is going to be fun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So we're gonna try? I mean, are we trying?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're trying to get pregnant. (They start kissing, but Chandler stops it.) Y'know I'm not really comfortable doing this in front of the babies. So, when do you want to start trying?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, hold on a sec.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Period math?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we could start trying. Now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, not here. Maybe here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait a minute, it's perfect. We got a lot of time to kill and we're in a building that's full of beds!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And it's so clean!!
They run off in search of a bed.
Commercial Break
The Vending Machines, Phoebe is buying a soda and Joey is shaking the candy machine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on you stupid machine! Come on!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it ate your money?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at her) No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I'll see you downstairs then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey I got one! I got one!
[Cut to the elevator lobby, Phoebe walks up and sees a man in a wheelchair with his broken leg extended.]

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh uh, up or down?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh down please. (The guy tries to reach the button, but can't.) I-I hate to be a ball buster can I just do it? (She pushes the button.)

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Could you press up too please?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! I feel so bad for you; I broke my leg once too.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh yeah? How'd yours happen?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it's a long story. It's kind of embarrassing. Let's just say there was a typographical error with a sex manual. (The guy laughs.) How about you?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Car accident.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh, let me guess some idiot on a cell phone wasn't paying attention?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Yeah. Me. (The elevator door opens.) Oh hey, that's me. (Rolls onto the elevator.) Hey uh, I take it you're just visiting someone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Well umm, if you have sometime y'know and maybe you might want to visit someone else...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah! I-I would like that.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: I'm in the middle... (The elevator door closes, cutting him off.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait! What?! No!! Elevator!! No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (standing behind her) Uh, you gotta press the button. (Does so.)
The Semi-Private Labor Room, Evil Bitch and Sick Bastard are gone and Ross has just finished talking to a nurse as Rachel stands and stretches.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The nurse said they're bringing in another woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, is she pregnant yet? She doesn't need to be; she'll still have the baby before I do. Oh Ross, another contraction! (Leans back on Ross for some support.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's it. That's it.
The next couple enters.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oooh, that sounded like a bad one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah it was.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Mine haven't been so bad. Oh! Here comes one now. (Hums then squeals a little bit.) Oh, that was a big one!
Rachel motions for Ross to close the privacy screen, which he does.
Another Waiting Room, Phoebe and Joey are trying to find out where the guy with the broken leg is.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the nurse) Excuse me? Could you help me with something? The patient I'm looking for has a broken leg and is in a wheelchair. And umm, he's like early to mid-thirties, very attractive.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I think I know who you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yay! Great! Okay, what room number is he in?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I'm sorry, that information is restricted to hospital staff...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (walks up) Uh, she's with me. (Introduces himself) Dr. Drake Remoray.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Dr. Drake who?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Remoray. It's Portuguese. We need that information; I'm a doctor.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: A doctor at this hospital?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damnit woman we're losing precious time! Now do you want this man's blood on your head?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hands.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hands! It is absolutely essential that you tell me what room the man my assistant described is staying in. He's a patient of mine, I've been treating him for years!

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: He's in room 816.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 816, thank you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you. (Starts to exit.)
Joey starts to leave, but stops.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And what is his name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (coming back for Joey) No! (Grabs Joey and drags him away.)
An Empty Hospital Room, Chandler and Monica enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think we found a place.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (They start kissing.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, wait! Do you want to set the mood a little?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Uh, we'll dim the lights, dim the lights. (He goes to the light switch and finds it's not a dimmer switch when he flips the lights off.) Or turn them out all together. Uh, no scented candles. Okay here. Here we go. (He sprays an aerosol air freshener above her.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Make me sterile, but okay.
He hops onto the bed and they start making out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Let's hurry-Oh wait! Do we have a condom? (He looks at her.) Oh right! (Laughs and they resume making out when a nurse catches them in the act.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, 98.6. You're gonna be fine.
Outside Room 816, Phoebe and Joey are approaching.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, this is it! (Looks in the window.) Oh, that's him! That's him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great! Go get him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait a second, or maybe you can go in first.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looks in the window) He's not really my type.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No not you, Dr. Drake Remoray. You can ask him questions and see what's he like. People tell doctors everything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But you said he was this great guy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But lately all the guys I meet seem really nice at first, then they turn out to be the biggest jerks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You do attract some stinkers.
The Semi-Private Labor Room, Dr. Long is checking on Rachel again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Dr. Long, I've been at this for seventeen hours! Three women have come and gone with their babies, you gotta give me some good news! How many centimeters am I dilated? Eight? Nine?

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Three.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just three?! I'm dilated three!

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: We are moving along, just slowly. (Rachel lies back and sighs.) Don't worry, you're doing great. I'll be back soon. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, y'know what? I'm not waiting! I'm gonna push this baby out! I'm doing it! I mean it's what? Three centimeters? That's gotta be like this! (Holds her hands a couple inches apart.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually it's more like this. (Pushes her hands to less than an inch apart.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh stupid metric system!
Another woman with a nurse and doctor enter, the woman is screaming.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Oh my. We're gonna need to take you straight to the delivery room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh for the love of God!

lines.speaker: Woman Giving Birth
lines.text: (yelling from the hallway) It's coming! It's coming!

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: And here it is! (The baby cries.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh come on!!
Room 816, Dr. Drake Remoray is entering.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi! I'm Dr. Drake Remoray and I have a few routine questions I need to ask you.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Really? I've been dealing with Dr. Wells.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, but I'm a neurologist. And just to be on the safe side, Dr. Wells wanted a more comprehensive overview of you status so he sent me.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Dr. Wells is a woman.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That was a test. Good response. All right, full name.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Clifford Burnett.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Date of birth?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: November 16th, 1968.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Age?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Can't you figure that out based on my date of birth?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm a doctor Cliff, not a mathematician.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. And uh, are you married.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh really? So, 33 and still single, would you say you have commitment issues?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Are all the questions this personal?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (checking the list) Yes.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Well uh if you must know I'm a widower.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that's terrible. I'm-I'm really sorry.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hmm. Do you sleep with women and never call them again?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Excellent! Excellent! And uh, finally, are you into any weird stuff y'know, sexually?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oooh, wrong answer. (Exits.)
The Semi-Private Labor Room, they're brining in yet another woman.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: (calling to the woman) This room's available.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! Okay wait! You listen to me! You listen to me! Since I have been waiting four women, that's four, one higher than the number of centimeters that I am dilated, have come and gone with their babies! I'm next! It's my turn! It's only fair! And if you bring in one woman and she has her baby before me I'm going to sue you! Not this hospital, I'm going to sue you! And my husband (Points at Ross) he's a lawyer!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh Rach...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Go get back on that case honey!

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I don't think the next patient is very far along.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well then bring her in.
Another nurse wheels the next pregnant woman in.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: OH....MY....GAWD!!! (Uh-huh, it's Janice.)
Ross and Rachel are, needless to say, stunned at the arrival of Janice.
Commercial Break
The Semi-Private Labor Room, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I....can't....believe this!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And yet somehow it's true!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I mean this is so great! We're gonna be baby buddies! (Does the laugh.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) Squeeze your legs together and cover the baby's ears!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: (entering, carrying a pillow) Hi sweetie!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Hi! Hi sweetheart! This is my husband Sid, I don't think you've met him. Ross, Rachel, this is Sid. I nabbed him a year ago at the dermatologist's office. Thank God for adult acne huh? (Does the laugh.)

lines.speaker: Sid
lines.text: I still can't believe it! I'm the luckiest guy in the world!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (softly) Really?

lines.speaker: Sid
lines.text: (To Janice) What'd he say?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh y'know what? You have to speak very loudly when you're talking to Sid, because he's almost completely deaf.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh there you go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I get it!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: So? Congratulations you two, I didn't even know you got married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh we-we didn't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no. We...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um uh...We're-we're just having this baby together but uh, uh that's all.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Why?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh well umm...we're just not in that place, y'know? But we're very excited about this.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh. Well then shut me up. (Does the laugh.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just tell me how.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Uh-oh, I feel another one coming. (She makes a sound like a goose during the contraction.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sid you lucky deaf bastard.
Outside Room 816, Joey is briefing Phoebe on Cliff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What else? What else?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, well he's 33.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Ah-uh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A widower.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He seemed like a stand up guy. Oh, and he's not into anything weird sexually.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Enter Pheebs.
Another Hospital Room, Chandler and Monica enter and start making out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Should we tell Rachel there's an empty private room right next door to hers?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We could, or we can have sex in it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well let me think about that, while I remove my pants!
They start making out again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (lying down on the bed) Okay mister! Fertilize me!
Suddenly they hear Janice laughing, and it ruins the moment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does that sound like Janice?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If it's not, then there's two of them. And that would mean it's the end of the world!
The Semi-Private Labor Room, Chandler and Monica are entering to see if they in fact did hear Janice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe this is taking so long. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh not bad. Do you know that feeling when you're trying to blow a Saint Bernard out your ass?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Weirdest thing. Did I hear-(A nurse opens the privacy screen and Chandler sees Janice)-Mother of God it's true!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler Bing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Jan-Janice!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not just Janice, Janice in labor, contracting and everything.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, this should be easy. I have a very wide pelvis. You remember Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice I didn't even know you were pregnant! Who's the unwitting human who's essence you've stolen?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: It's you. This is yours.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (laughs) Look how nervous he gets! We haven't slept together in years! (Laughs again.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's funny. Does it-does it hurt? Does the labor hurt?
Room 816, Phoebe is making her move on Cliff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay I've got one for you, if you had too which one would you rather eat, a seeing eye dog or a talking gorilla?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: I'd have to say...the talking gorilla, because at least I can explain to him that you're making me eat him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Somebody went to college. Wow. (Cliff gets uncomfortable) What is it? I'm sorry. (She moves her arm, which was resting on the same pillow his leg is.)

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: No, I'm sorry. It's just my foot itches like crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'll get it. (She gets up and grabs a spoon.)

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Wow! I usually get to know a girl a little better before I let her spoon me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Relax, it's not like we're forking.
The Semi-Private Labor Room, Janice is being moved to the delivery room and is screaming in pain.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh that's five Ross. Five women have had five babies! And I have had no babies! Why doesn't she want to come out?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what I think it is? I think you've made such a nice home for her over the last nine months that she just doesn't want to leave.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Look at you making up crap for me. Oh God! (Starts another contraction as Dr. Long enters.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Twenty-one hours, you're a hero.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Doctor you gotta do something! I think you gotta give me drugs or you gotta light a fire up in there and just smoke it out.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Actually, I think you're ready to go to the delivery room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Ten centimeters, you're about to become a mom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My God. Okay. (Another woman enters.) Ha-ha-ha beat ya! Sucker!
Room 816, Phoebe and Cliff are eating some pudding with spoons.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Is this the same spoon that was in my cast? (Smells it.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what? This one is. (Eats another spoonful of pudding as Cliff sees something on TV.)

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: Oh my God! That's the doctor who was in my room before!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh. Okay, Mr. Perkazet.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: I'm telling you! The guy from that show was here in my room, asking me all these weird questions!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Cliff, do you really believe that a character from a TV show was here in your room?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Rachel's having her baby!! (Phoebe turns and looks at him.) Which is of no interest to me, I'm a neurologist.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: That-that's him! You know him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Okay. I-Okay umm...this...I-I sent my friend Joey in here to find out stuff about you. Umm y'know, if it helps you came off great. A lot better than I'm coming off right now.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: I don't believe this. You got him to pretend he was some fake doctor?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fake? Excuse me? Hello? (Taps the TV screen.)

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: And then you tried to make me think that I was crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're right, that was wrong. I'm sorry. I'm so sorry. It's just that I liked you so much. Can we just, can we just start over?

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, if I may? Umm-umm look, Cliff, you told me a lot of personal stuff about you, right? And maybe-maybe it would if-if would help if-if you knew some personal stuff about her. Uh, she was married to a gay ice dancer. Uh, she gave birth to her brother's triplets. Oh! Oh! Her-her twin sister used to do porn!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh Joey, we're trying to dial down the crazy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, look we don't, we don't really know each other so it would be really easy to just forget about this, but there seems to be something between us. And I don't know about you but that doesn't happen to me a lot.

lines.speaker: Cliff
lines.text: It doesn't happen to me either.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me neither.
The Delivery Room, Rachel is finally giving birth.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Push. Push. Come on push for five seconds. 5...4...

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Okay, the next contraction should be in about twenty seconds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't. I can't push anymore, I can't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie you're doing great.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God twenty seconds my ass!!

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Here we go! Okay, keep pushing! Wait! I see something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? You do? You do? (Looks) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't say, "Oh my God!" Oh my God what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: It's the baby's buttock, she's breech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh thank God, I thought she had two heads.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God. Is she gonna be okay?

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: She's gonna be fine. Okay, she's in a more difficult position so you're gonna have to push even harder now. Go! Push!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Go!

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Rachel you're gonna have to push even harder, nothing's happening!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, I can't!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes you can!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Hey! Come on! You can! I know you can do this! Let's go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't. Please, you do it for me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! Come on let's-One more time! One final push! Ready? 1...2...3! (Rachel pushes so hard her head snaps up head-butting Ross and knocking him down.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Good!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (from the floor) Keep pushing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You have no idea how much this hurts. (All of the women in the room turn and glare at him.) Keep going! Keep going!

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Here we go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Oh! She's upside down but she's coming! She's coming!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Oh my God oh! Oh my God she's here.
The newest friend cries.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh she's...she's perfect.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, she's so tiny. (Starts crying) Where'd she go?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh it's okay. They're just-they're just wrapping her up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Well be careful with her, she's really tiny.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Here she is!
Dr. Long hands her to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hey you. Thanks for coming out of me. (The baby cries.) I know. Oh. Yeah. Oh, she's looking at me. Hi! I know you.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Do we have a name yet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: That's fine, for now we'll just call her Baby Girl Green.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, Baby Girl Geller-Green.
Ross and Rachel look into each other's eyes and kiss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello baby girl.
Commercial Break
The Recovery Room, Ross is taking pictures of Rachel holding the baby as the rest of the gang enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can we come in?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come in.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There she is!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, she's so beautiful.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here. (Hands her to Monica.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! She's amazing. Oh, oh I'm so glad you guys got drunk and had sex!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's incredible, I mean one minute she's inside you and then 47 hours later here she is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (taking the baby) She looks so real! (The gang looks at him.) Y'know what I mean! She's this whole tiny little person. She already has eyelashes and knees and...uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no, no for I second there I counted six fingers, but one was from the other hand so we're good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, my turn. My turn. (Joey hands her to Phoebe.) Oh! You're so cute! Oh, I could squeeze your little head! (Pause) I won't.
Rachel starts crying again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh nothing I... Sorry, I just can't stop crying.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The doctor says it's completely normal with all the hormones. Plus, you-you're sleep deprived.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So? You guys are all sleep deprived. I don't see you weeping because you put your slippers on the wrong feet. Oh God. (Starts to cry harder.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the matter now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I was reliving it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh. (Hands her back to Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, do you know what you're gonna call her yet?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute it's not gonna be Baby Girl? I thought that was so original!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh actually, we-we've narrowed it down to two names.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and y'know what? I love them both, so why don't you just pick one and that'll be it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Umm, okay uh...everyone...this...is Isabella. (Rachel starts crying.) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's not her name! I'm sorry, she just doesn't feel like an Isabella.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So then I guess Ferdinad is out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What was the other one Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, Delilah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh great! Suddenly she sounds like a biblical whore.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I guess we're back to uh, Baby Girl.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay honey, you'll find a name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, easy for you to say, you already know what your kids names are going to be.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I've had them picked out since I was fourteen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, it's gonna be named after some snack or baked good isn't it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well tell us! What are they?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, okay. If it's a boy it's Daniel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And if it's a girl?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't want to say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, just tell us! We're not gonna want it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. It's Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Emma! (Looks at the baby and starts to cry.) See? I don't want it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Take it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's clearly an Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, but you love that name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but I love you more. Besides y'know, nothing goes with Bing. So I'm screwed. I mean... (Rachel hands Emma to Monica.) Oh, hi Emma. Yeah, that's you. You're our little Em. Oh what's that honey? What? Oh, you want a little cousin? (To Chandler) You want a cousin right now?!
A Janitorial Closet, Monica and Chandler are emerging slowly.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was amazing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. Hey, do you realize we may have just changed our lives forever? We may have just started a family. Nine months from now we can be here, having our own baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And if not, we got to do it on a bucket.
The Recovery Room, Rachel is putting Emma down for a nap.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (entering) Yoo-hoo! Aaron Litman-Neurolic would like to say hello to his future bride.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh! (Looks at Aaron and recoils in horror.) Wow! He kinda takes your breath away doesn't he?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: He's a keeper. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm fine. (Gasps in pain as she sits down.)

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Can I just say, I really admire what you're doing. Just raising her all alone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm not doing it alone. I have Ross.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, sure. Now. But what happens when he meets somebody else and gets married?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then he gets a divorce, it's Ross!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I'm telling you Rachel, listen to Janice. They all say they're gonna be there until they start their real family.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I-That's never gonna happen with Ross.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh well that's what I thought about my first husband, now I'm lucky if my kid gets to spend the weekend with her father and the twins and little Ms. New Boobs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I hate to be the one to say it, but honey you two (Her and Emma) are on your own.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well... That's...y'know-That's-We've been alone for the last twenty minutes we're doing okay. Besides y'know what? I-I-Maybe we won't be alone, 'cause lately I-I-things have been happening between me and Ross, y'know? Right before I went into labor, we-we had this kiss. Y'know? So it might be the...the beginning of something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey Janice!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who's this little guy? (Gasps when he sees Aaron.)

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Say hello to Aaron, your future son-in-law.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no. No.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I'm gonna leave the three of you alone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Man! Did you see the kid on that nose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh. (Ross takes off his coat and sets in on a chair.) Y'know what I was, I was thinking about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm...that kiss before we left the apartment. That was some-something huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, it really was. But we...we gotta be careful. We...we can't let that happen again, y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pause) Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean we don't want to go down that road do we?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No, of course not. No. That's why I brought it up. (Pause) They didn't have any sodas?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! I'm sorry, I was talking to this nurse, completely forgot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's all right. (He goes to get her a soda.) And so it begins.
Outside the Nursery, Ross is looking at Emma as Phoebe walks up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is she in there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. She's putting her down now, that's her. (Points to the nurse putting Emma now.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at Emma!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just can't decide who she looks more alike, you or Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh what are you kidding? She's gorgeous, it's all Rachel.
Pause

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, for the last time, why aren't you two together again? (Silence from Ross.) No, I know. I know, because you're not in that place. Which would be fine, except you totally are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's...it's complicated okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah that's true. Yeah, you love her. You always have. You have a child together. There is no right answer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, we've been together. Okay? And then apart, and then together, and then apart, and now we have a baby. (Pause) It's just if-if we got together again and it didn't work out...I could never do that to Emma. I mean she-she thinking everything-(Starts to cry.) Oh that's...now me. What do they put something in the water in this place? Since Rachel and I we're doing really, we're doing really well right now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. I know. I know. I know, and if you try to make it more you might wreck it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, exactly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. (Pause) Or you might get everything you've wanted since you were fifteen.
The Delivery Room, Rachel is in bed as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. I just saw a woman breast feeding both of her twins at the same time; it is like a freak show up here. (Notices she's wiping her eyes.) What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is it? Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really it's nothing. I'm just...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach come on, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I've just been thinking about how my baby and I are gonna be all alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you talking about alone? What about Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, he'll be with his real family, the twins and little miss new boobs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, how long was I watching that woman?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm just saying that y'know, someday Ross is gonna meet somebody and...he's gonna have his own life. Right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just never thought I would raise this baby all by myself. Pretty dumb huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, listen to me, listen to me...you are never ever gonna be alone. Okay? I promise that's not gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey. Honey what would I do without you?
They hug.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't have to worry about that okay?
Pause

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hon can you grab me my other box of tissues? They're right on that chair under Ross's coat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

He moves Ross's coat to get the tissues and the engagement ring box Mrs. Geller gave him falls out of the pocket it was inside. Joey goes to one knee, picks up the box, opens it, and sees that it's an engagement ring.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My God.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.
He turns to face Rachel on one knee with the box open.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (seeing the ring) Oh my God. (Pause) Okay.
Joey is stunned.
[Cut to Ross getting of an elevator carrying a bouquet of flowers and walking down the hall to Rachel's room.]
[Fade to black.]
Closing Credits

{Transcriber's Note: As with all the cliffhangers, there was no credits scene. There will be a ninth and final season of Friends starting sometime in September. See you then, have a good summer everyone.}
End
Season 8
